mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the federal funds rate raises the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, mechanically compressing S&P 500 valuations and generating negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rate increases are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate crossing above 4.0 percent signals a deteriorating labor market and elevated recession probability, prompting investors to de-risk equity portfolios over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Positive 60-day forward returns occur in more than 55 percent of triggered months, invalidating the recession risk channel.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent monthly declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate a broad-based contraction in consumer demand, reducing corporate earnings expectations and driving negative equity returns.
failure_condition: The joint decline of retail sales and housing starts fails to predict negative 20-day returns with statistical significance (p > 0.05) in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production growth during early economic recovery phases signals strengthening corporate output and margins, generating positive short-term equity momentum.
failure_condition: The signal loses predictive power with Rank IC below 0.01 when tested exclusively outside post-shock recovery regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index levels exceeding 305.0 trigger expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity multiples and producing negative forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 60 percent of high-inflation months, contradicting the tightening expectation channel.

mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payroll employment combined with a declining unemployment rate indicates robust labor market absorption, supporting corporate revenue growth and positive equity returns.
failure_condition: The interaction term between payroll growth and unemployment decline becomes statistically insignificant in out-of-sample regression testing.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth directly correlates with stronger corporate revenue expectations and improved profit margins, generating positive short-term equity momentum across market cycles.
failure_condition: Five-day forward returns show no significant correlation with quarterly GDP growth rates, with p-values consistently exceeding 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-rate environment, further increases in the federal funds rate trigger liquidity drainage from risk assets and higher borrowing costs, causing negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Positive returns dominate during rate hikes above 4.5 percent, with a win rate exceeding 55 percent over a 20-day horizon.
